name: loop_slice_ngon
description: Loop Slice 'Slice N-gon' option (task 0250; watertight-by-default 0265). Planar horizontal strip: two left quads Q0=[0,1,6,5], Q1=[1,2,7,6], a HEXAGON H=[2,10,3,8,11,7] whose top+bottom are split at x=2.5 (verts 10,11), then a right quad Q2=[3,4,9,8]. The seed edge is the vertical edge (1,0,0)-(1,1,0) shared by Q0 and Q1. The quad ring walks LEFT via Q0 to the boundary and RIGHT via Q1 into the hexagon. With Slice N-gon OFF (default) the ring TERMINATES at the hexagon (walkRingSide stops at the non-quad) — only Q0,Q1 are sliced (3 midpoints). The terminating midpoint (2,0.5) is ABSORBED into the hexagon by default (H becomes the 7-gon [2,10,3,8,11,7,14]), so the cut is WATERTIGHT: 15 verts / 20 edges / 6 faces; the exit edge (2,0)-(2,1) into the hexagon is GONE (replaced by its two half-edges), NO T-junction. With Slice N-gon ON the ring CROSSES the hexagon: it enters via its left vertical edge and exits via the opposite (right vertical) edge, splitting the hexagon by the chord between the two edge midpoints, then continues into Q2. All four faces are sliced (5 midpoints), so 17 verts / 24 edges / 8 faces; the full edges (2,0)-(2,1) and (3,0)-(3,1) are gone (replaced by their half-edges) — the crossing is watertight. Reference-independent geometry law: every new vertex sits at the 0.5 midpoint of a pre-op vertical edge (0/1/2/3/4, x=0.5 apart). The countable OFF/ON difference (2 extra midpoints, 2 extra faces, the two exit rails reached) is the proof the ring traversed the n-gon.
source: LIVE-CAPTURED (task 0263). The `ngon` behaviour was driven HEADLESSLY on the reference app (same hexagon strip; capture provenance in the private toolcards/mesh.loopSlice). ngon ON MATCHES this fixture BIT-EXACT: 17 verts / 24 edges / 8 faces, new midpoints at x=0,1,2,3,4 — the ring crosses the hexagon and reaches Q2, watertight. ngon OFF now ALSO MATCHES the reference BIT-EXACT: the reference default keeps the terminating cut WATERTIGHT by absorbing the terminating midpoint into the hexagon (7-gon [2,10,3,8,11,7,14], 20 edges), and vibe3d's default was changed to match (task 0265 — the absorb of a terminating midpoint at a non-quad/border face is now the default in Mesh.insertEdgeLoopsMulti, no longer gated on Keep Quads / Slice Selected). Captured values: faceCount 6, vertexCount 15, 20 edges (ls_der_ngon_off.json). The ON exit-edge rule (entry -> entryJ+N/2) is confirmed by the live crossing.

provenance.notes: Driven headlessly on the reference app (same hexagon strip).
